summary.scholar: On April 14, 1865, five days after Lees surrender, Lincoln was shot by John Wilkes Booth at Fords Theatre at approximately 10:15 p.m. He died the following morning at 7:22 a.m. Andrew Johnson became the seventeenth president.
summary.explorer: Just five days after the war ended, President Lincoln went to see a play. A man named John Wilkes Booth shot him. Lincoln died the next morning.

significance.scholar: Lincolns assassination made him the first U.S. president murdered in office and profoundly complicated Reconstruction.
significance.explorer: Lincoln was the first president ever to be killed. His death made bringing the country back together much harder.
